tasks.title: feat(database): implement resource plane Postgres schema with TimescaleDB
acceptance_criteria: database/init/00-create-databases.sh creates dora_metrics, infisical, defectdojo databases with least-privilege roles via psql
Script is idempotent (safe to re-run)
database/init/01-dora-schema.sql creates all tables: event_queue, raw_events, dora_snapshots, archetype_history, wellbeing_surveys, vsi_stage_breakdown
database/init/02-dora-roles.sql creates dora_app role with least-privilege grants
database/timescaledb/hypertables.sql converts time-series tables to TimescaleDB hypertables
database/migrations/001-initial-schema.sql matches init scripts (first run = migration 001)
tests/unit/test_schema.py verifies tables exist, hypertable creation, role permissions (not superuser)
docker-compose.dev.yml includes local TimescaleDB container for solo development
All tests pass in CI
tasks.description: Define the PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB schema for all uFawkesDORA data: event queue, raw events, DORA snapshots, archetype history, wellbeing surveys, and VSM stage breakdown. This is the foundational data model — everything else reads from or writes to these tables.

tasks.title: feat(events): define canonical event schemas for all five DORA metrics
acceptance_criteria: events/deployment-event.schema.json defines required fields: schema_version, event_type ("deployment"), repo, service, environment, commit_sha, deployed_at (ISO8601), status ("success" | "failed" | "rollback"), pipeline_url; optional: deploy_duration_seconds, ai_assisted (bool)

events/incident-event.schema.json defines required: schema_version, event_type ("incident"), incident_id, repo, service, status ("opened" | "resolved"), occurred_at; optional: linked_deployment_sha, severity

events/pr-event.schema.json defines required: schema_version, event_type ("pr"), repo, pr_number, commit_sha, status ("opened" | "merged" | "closed"), occurred_at, first_commit_at; optional: lines_added, lines_deleted, ai_assisted (bool)
events/rework-event.schema.json defines required: schema_version, event_type ("rework"), repo, deployment_sha, rework_type ("hotfix" | "rollback" | "patch"), triggered_at, user_visible (bool)
All schemas include version field starting at "1.0" with documented versioning policy in events/README.md
tests/unit/test_event_schemas.py validates each schema using jsonschema; tests both valid and invalid payloads
events/README.md provides field reference, versioning policy, and guidance on distinguishing rework vs normal deployment
tasks.description: Define the JSON schemas that all event sources must conform to. These schemas are the API contract between any CI/CD system and uFawkesDORA. Stability of these schemas is critical — a schema change breaks all connected collectors.

tasks.title: feat(ingestion): stateless ingestion API with Postgres event queue
acceptance_criteria: ingestion/api/main.py exposes FastAPI app with POST /event (validates schema, enqueues, returns {"queued": true, "id": N}), GET /health (returns {"status": "ok", "queue_depth": N}), and POST /event/batch
ingestion/api/validator.py validates event_type field first, then routes to correct schema; returns structured 422 with field-level errors
ingestion/api/queue.py implements enqueue with INSERT INTO event_queue (event_type, payload) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING id; connection pool via asyncpg

ingestion/processor/worker.py implements dequeue loop: SELECT id, payload FROM event_queue WHERE status='pending' ORDER BY received_at LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED; writes to raw_events, pushes OTel counter, pushes Loki log, updates status='done'; on failure increments attempts, updates status='failed' if attempts >= 3
ingestion/Dockerfile uses multi-stage build, non-root user, includes health check
tests/unit/test_ingestion_api.py covers valid event accepted (201), invalid schema rejected (422 with field errors), queue depth in health check, duplicate events handled
tests/unit/test_worker.py covers dequeue success path, dequeue failure path (attempts increment), SKIP LOCKED (two workers don't claim same row)

Evidence: curl -X POST http://localhost:8088/event -d @fixtures/deployment-success.json → {"queued": true, "id": 1} → row appears in event_queue with status 'done' → row appears in raw_events → OTel span visible in uFawkesObs Tempo
tasks.description: Implement the stateless FastAPI ingestion API that accepts events on POST /event, validates the canonical schema, and enqueues to the Postgres event_queue table using the SKIP LOCKED pattern. This is the only component that needs to be reachable from external event sources.

tasks.title: feat(compute): five DORA metrics + Rework Rate from PostgreSQL
acceptance_criteria: compute/metrics.py CLI: python compute/metrics.py --window 30 --repo paruff/uFawkesObs
Writes result to dora_snapshots table
Pushes Prometheus metrics via pushgateway: dora_deployment_frequency_per_week, dora_lead_time_p50_hours, dora_lead_time_p95_hours, dora_fdrt_p50_hours, dora_cfr_pct, dora_rework_rate_pct
proxy_metrics: true flag set when first_commit_at unavailable (falls back to PR merge time as lead time proxy); flag propagated to dora_snapshots
FDRT computed as deployment-gap, NOT incident-resolution gap (documented in inline comments with DORA 2025 citation)
DORA tier thresholds from 2025 research applied per metric; each metric in output includes dora_tier field
tests/unit/test_metrics.py covers all five metrics with fixture data; includes FDRT edge cases (no recovery deployment in window → null, not error)
GitHub Actions cron job: .github/workflows/compute-metrics.yml runs daily
tasks.description: Implement compute/metrics.py — the reference computation of all five DORA delivery metrics from the Postgres raw_events table, using TimescaleDB functions for time-series aggregation. Output to dora_snapshots table AND pushgateway (for Grafana time-series panels and alerting).

tasks.title: feat(collectors): GitHub Actions reusable workflows for deployment and PR events
acceptance_criteria: collectors/github/dora-deployment-event.yml is reusable workflow (workflow_call) with inputs: status, environment, pipeline_url, ai_assisted (optional, default false); emits deployment-event to ${vars.DORA_INGESTION_URL}/event
collectors/github/dora-pr-event.yml is reusable workflow; emits pr-event on PR merge including first_commit_at (oldest commit SHA timestamp in the PR, fetched via GitHub API)
collectors/github/README.md explains how to wire both workflows in 10 minutes: "add these 3 lines to your release workflow"
collectors/github/example-consumer.yml shows complete example workflow with both collector calls in context
tests/unit/test_github_collector.py validates schema compliance for sample GitHub webhook payloads
Evidence: wire to uFawkesObs repo, trigger a test release → deployment event appears in raw_events table
tasks.description: Create reusable GitHub Actions workflows that any repo in the fawkes suite can call to emit deployment events and PR events to the uFawkesDORA ingestion API. This is the primary event source for GitHub-native teams.

tasks.title: feat(collectors): generic webhook receiver + Woodpecker snippet + manual incident
acceptance_criteria: collectors/woodpecker/pipeline-snippet.yml is Woodpecker pipeline step that POSTs a deployment-event on pipeline success/failure; uses from_secret for DORA_INGESTION_URL and DORA_API_KEY (if auth enabled)
collectors/generic/curl-examples.sh provides curl commands for each event type (deployment success, deployment failed, incident opened, incident resolved, rework); well-commented, copy-paste ready
collectors/manual-incident/declare-incident.sh prompts for incident_id and service, then POSTs the incident-event; companion resolve-incident.sh for resolution
collectors/generic/README.md documents how to wire Portainer webhook (Portainer supports outgoing webhooks on stack redeploy)
Evidence: run declare-incident.sh → incident event appears in raw_events
tasks.description: Define three collector patterns for non-GitHub sources: a Woodpecker CI pipeline snippet, a generic curl-based webhook pattern for any CI/CD system, and a manual incident declaration template for teams without PagerDuty/Grafana OnCall.

tasks.title: docs: README following uFawkes documentation standard
acceptance_criteria: All 9 required sections present: What This Is, What This Is Not, Status, Architecture, Quick Start, Testing, DORA Capability, Contributing, Suite Context
Architecture section explains the plane model: uFawkesDORA is a stateless compute plane that attaches to a resource plane Postgres instance
Includes architecture diagram (ASCII or Mermaid) showing the two-plane model
"Status" section honest about current state: "v0.0 — scaffold only. See roadmap for what's coming."
Suite Context section includes links to all 8 repos in the fawkes platform suite
docs-lint CI check passes
tasks.description: Write a README following the minimum uFawkes documentation standard. The architecture section must explain the two-plane model where uFawkesDORA is a stateless compute plane attached to a resource plane Postgres instance.

tasks.title: feat(dashboards): DORA Overview — five metrics with 2025 tier model
acceptance_criteria: dashboards/dora-overview.json is valid Grafana provisioning JSON
Five metric panels; each shows: stat (current value + tier badge), time-series (30/90 day trend), threshold lines (2025 DORA research values)
FDRT panel uses Throughput framing (not "recovery time from incident") with inline annotation explaining the 2025 reclassification
Rework Rate panel includes tooltip: "Only counts user-visible unplanned deployments"
Dashboard variables: team, service, environment, window (7d/30d/90d)
Banner when proxy_metrics=true: "⚠ Proxy metrics active — wire deployment events for accurate data. See collectors/README.md"
Documented copy step: dashboards/dora-overview.json → uFawkesObs grafana/provisioning/dashboards/
Evidence: docker compose up with demo fixture data → all five panels show non-empty data; no Grafana errors in browser console
tasks.description: Create the primary Grafana dashboard showing the five DORA metrics with current values, 30-day trends, DORA 2025 tier overlays, and team/service/window filters. Uses both Prometheus (time-series) and Postgres (snapshots) datasources.

tasks.title: feat(dashboards): Leading Indicators — early warning before metrics degrade
acceptance_criteria: dashboards/leading-indicators.json is valid Grafana provisioning JSON
All five leading indicator panels with annotation text explaining what degradation means and which lagging DORA metric it predicts
PR size trend panel includes note: "AI-assisted PRs average 50-150% larger than baseline — monitor for Rework Rate correlation (DORA 2025)"
Branch age panel notes limitation if GitHub API polling not available; suggests alternative: use pr-event opened_at - first_commit_at as proxy
Annotations map each leading indicator to its predicted lagging DORA metric
Dashboard uses PostgreSQL datasource only (no Prometheus for leading indicators)
tasks.description: Create a PostgreSQL-only Grafana dashboard for leading indicators that predict DORA metric degradation before it appears in the five metrics. Includes PR cycle time, PR size trend, CI duration trend, rework rate trend, and branch age as predictors of Lead Time, Deployment Frequency, CFR, and Rework Rate changes.

tasks.title: feat(alerts): regression-based Alertmanager rules (not threshold-based)
acceptance_criteria: alerts/dora-regression.yaml contains all five regression alerts: DoraDeploymentFrequencyDrop, DoraLeadTimeIncrease, DoraFDRTSpike, DoraCFRSpike, DoraReworkRateClimb
alerts/leading-indicator.yaml contains PR cycle time + PR size trend alerts
Each alert includes summary, description (with current value and 30-day baseline for context), and runbook_url linking to docs/runbooks/
tests/unit/test_alert_rules.py uses promtool test rules with synthetic data: each alert fires on degraded data, does NOT fire on healthy data
Alerts wired to Alertmanager in uFawkesObs via docker-compose.yml network connection
Alert category labels: dora_throughput for metrics 1-3, dora_stability for metrics 4-5
tasks.description: Implement Prometheus alerting rules that fire when a DORA metric degrades relative to its 30-day baseline (regression-based) rather than when it crosses a fixed threshold. Includes alerts for the five DORA metrics plus leading indicators.

tasks.title: feat(compute): seven archetype classifier with wellbeing integration
acceptance_criteria: compute/archetype.py inputs: repo name + optional quarter string (pulls from dora_snapshots and wellbeing_surveys automatically)
Returns: {"archetype": "Pragmatic performers", "confidence": 0.78, "wellbeing_data": false, "primary_bottleneck": "review_cycle_time", "recommendations": ["...", "..."]}
When wellbeing_surveys has no data for the period: returns metrics-only classification with confidence capped at 0.65 and wellbeing_data: false
Classification logic documented inline — not a black box; cites DORA 2025 archetype definitions for each decision boundary
Writes result to archetype_history table
compute/archetype_survey.md provides four-question quarterly wellbeing survey with curl command to POST results to ingestion API
tests/unit/test_archetype.py covers all seven archetypes with representative fixture data; tests confidence degradation without wellbeing data
Scheduled via .github/workflows/compute-archetype.yml (monthly, after compute-metrics.yml runs)
tasks.description: Implement compute/archetype.py to classify teams into one of the seven 2025 DORA archetypes using delivery metrics from dora_snapshots and optional wellbeing scores from wellbeing_surveys. Store classification in archetype_history table.

tasks.title: feat(dashboards): Archetype Profile + AI Impact dashboards
acceptance_criteria: dashboards/archetype-profile.json is valid Grafana provisioning JSON
Archetype name stat panel (prominent)
Radar chart: five metrics normalized 0-1, overlaid with archetype centroid
Confidence stat panel with colour: green (≥0.75), yellow (0.5-0.75), red (<0.5)
"Confidence limited — no wellbeing survey data" text panel when confidence < 0.65
Two recommendation text panels keyed to archetype (static text; no LLM calls)
Link to compute/archetype_survey.md
dashboards/ai-impact.json is valid Grafana provisioning JSON
PR size 14-day MA panel with DORA 2025 reference annotation: "AI inflates PR size 50-150% — monitor Rework Rate for quality signal"
Rework Rate vs Deployment Frequency time series (dual-axis): "faster but messier" quadrant
Code churn rate panel (lines changed within 14 days of original commit / total lines)
FDRT trend panel
ai_assisted annotation line when available
tasks.description: Create two Postgres-dependent Grafana dashboards: Archetype Profile (shows team's current archetype, confidence, radar chart vs archetype centroid, recommendations) and AI Impact (shows PR size trend vs deployment frequency, code churn rate, Rework Rate vs Deployment Frequency scatter, FDRT trend with AI adoption overlay).

tasks.title: feat(notifications): weekly Slack digest + regression alerts to Slack
acceptance_criteria: notifications/digest/generate_digest.py queries latest dora_snapshots from Postgres, produces structured weekly digest
Digest content (per repo): five metrics current vs prior week, ✅/⚠️/❌ per metric, one "Focus this week" recommendation (worst-trending metric), Grafana link
Markdown output: notifications/digest/weekly-digest-YYYY-WW.md
notifications/slack/slack_webhook.py posts digest to SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL; gracefully skips with logged warning if not configured
.github/workflows/weekly-digest.yml runs Monday 8am UTC; manual dispatch also supported
Alertmanager receivers block in uFawkesObs routes dora_regression and leading_indicator alerts to DORA_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL (distinct from other alert channels)
Alert Slack message format: metric name, current value, 30d baseline, trend emoji, runbook link — NOT a wall of text
Evidence: inject a metric regression via synthetic Prometheus data → alert fires → Slack message received within 5 minutes
tasks.description: Implement two notification channels: a weekly Markdown digest posted to Slack (or saved as file) summarizing DORA metrics, and Slack routing for regression alerts from Alertmanager to a dedicated DORA alerts channel.

tasks.title: feat(notifications): PR-level lead time annotation
acceptance_criteria: notifications/pr_annotation/annotate.py reads PR event from Postgres, computes this PR's lead time, queries 30d P50 baseline, generates comment
.github/workflows/dora-pr-annotation.yml is reusable workflow, runs on PR merge
Comment format: lead time for this PR, team 30d P50, emoji (🚀 if faster, ⏱️ if slower), one-line note if >2x P50 ("consider breaking this into smaller PRs")
If ai_assisted: true: adds "AI-assisted PR: lead time vs baseline tracked for AI impact dashboard"
Disabled via DORA_PR_ANNOTATIONS: "false" repo variable
Posted by github-actions[bot], not a personal access token
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Action that posts a comment on every merged PR with its lead time contribution versus the team's 30-day P50 baseline, including coaching notes and AI-assisted tracking.

tasks.title: feat(compute): value stream indicators — stage-level lead time breakdown
acceptance_criteria: compute/vsi.py queries raw_events for pr-events and deployment-events, reconstructs stage durations for each commit-to-deploy journey
Writes to vsi_stage_breakdown table
Computes: value-add time (coding + CI + deploy), wait time (review + queue), VSM efficiency % (value-add / total)
Identifies primary bottleneck (highest wait time stage) with repo-level output
dashboards/value-stream.json is valid Grafana provisioning JSON showing stage breakdown waterfall + bottleneck highlight + efficiency trend
tests/unit/test_vsi.py with representative event fixture sequences
tasks.description: Implement compute/vsi.py to calculate Value Stream Indicators that break down lead time into stages (coding, review, CI, deploy, rework) to identify constraints and where AI productivity gains are being absorbed. Includes corresponding Grafana dashboard.

tasks.title: docs(dojo): Yellow belt curriculum + Dojo integration
acceptance_criteria: dojo-content/01-yellow-belt/dora-fundamentals.md explains the five DORA metrics and why they matter
dojo-content/01-yellow-belt/plane-model.md details the two-plane architecture (compute vs resource plane)
dojo-content/01-yellow-belt/event-schemas.md covers canonical event formats and how to emit them from CI/CD systems
dojo-content/01-yellow-belt/dashboards-overview.md walks through DORA Overview and Leading Indicators dashboards
dojo-content/01-yellow-belt/alerts-notifications.md explains regression-based alerting and notification channels
dojo-content/01-yellow-belt/hands-on-lab.md provides step-by-step guide to emit events and observe them in the system
All Dojo content follows uFawkes documentation standards with clear learning objectives and exercises
Content is discoverable via the uFawkesDojo navigation structure
tasks.description: Create Dojo educational content for Yellow Belt certification covering DORA fundamentals, the two-plane architecture, event schemas, dashboards, alerts, and hands-on labs. Content should be modular and integrate with the existing uFawkesDojo platform.
